error.signature: Confluence API content renders incorrectly or loses formatting when using wrong format type
error.regex: (Confluence.*storage.*format|Confluence.*editor.*format|XHTML.*storage.*body|Confluence.*rendering.*broken|Confluence\ API\ content\ renders\ incorrectly\ or\ loses\ formatti)

verdict.summary: Confluence has two content formats: 'storage' (XHTML-based, what the API returns) and 'editor' (what users see). They are NOT interchangeable. Posting editor format to storage API corrupts content. Macros use storage format exclusively.

dead_ends.action: Post HTML directly to Confluence API body.storage.value
dead_ends.why_fails: Confluence storage format is NOT regular HTML. It uses custom XHTML with ac: and ri: namespaces for macros. Regular HTML tags may work but macros, tables, and mentions require storage format.

dead_ends.action: Read content from API and display directly as HTML in another app
dead_ends.why_fails: Storage format contains Confluence-specific XHTML (ac:structured-macro, ri:attachment). Rendering this as HTML in a non-Confluence context shows broken markup.

workarounds.action: Use Confluence REST API's body.view for rendered HTML, body.storage for editing
workarounds.how: GET /content/123?expand=body.view  # rendered HTML for display; body.storage for programmatic editing

workarounds.action: Use the /contentbody/convert endpoint to transform between formats
Use Atlassian Document Format (ADF) for Confluence Cloud v2 API
workarounds.how: Confluence Cloud v2 API uses ADF (JSON) instead of XHTML storage format. Check API version before choosing format.
